title: Largest & Smallest

content.problemStatement:
Write a program to find both the largest and smallest number in a list of integers.


Input Format
In first line you should take size of the list (or array). 

In second line you should take elements of the array.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the list; the second line contains N space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case print two lines. The first line should be "Largest: X" where X is the maximum value in the list. The second line should be "Smallest: Y" where Y is the minimum value in the list.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The single test case contains five numbers; the maximum is 78 and the minimum is 12.
Among the six numbers the largest is 99 and the smallest is 3.
With only one element it is both the largest and the smallest.

hidden.label: stress case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Largest: 10
Smallest: 10
Largest: 5
Smallest: -5
Largest: 3
Smallest: 1
Largest: 4
Smallest: 1
Largest: 0
Smallest: 0

hidden.label: stress case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
Largest: 0
Smallest: -2
Largest: 1000
Smallest: -1000
Largest: 7
Smallest: 7

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the N numbers once while maintaining two variables: current maximum and current minimum. Initialise both with the first element, then for every subsequent element update the maximum if the element is larger and update the minimum if it is smaller. After processing the whole list we output the stored maximum and minimum. This single pass gives O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i is the test case index (0‑based). Use RINT(a,b) for random int, POWER(a,b) for a**b.
    # The function should print the complete input for the i‑th hidden test case.
    if i == 0:
        print(1)            # T
        print(1)            # N
        print(-1000)        # single minimum value
    elif i == 1:
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(0)
    elif i == 2:
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(1000)
    elif i == 3:
        print(1)
        print(2)
        print(-1000, 1000)
    elif i == 4:
        print(1)
        print(15)
        print(' '.join(['1000']*15))
    elif i == 5:
        print(1)
        print(15)
        print(' '.join(['-1000']*15))
    elif i == 6:
        print(1)
        print(15)
        seq = []
        for j in range(15):
            seq.append(str(-1000 if j%2==0 else 1000))
        print(' '.join(seq))
    elif i == 7:
        print(1)
        print(15)
        vals = [999,-999,998,-998,997,-997,996,-996,995,-995,994,-994,993,-993,992]
        print(' '.join(map(str, vals)))
    elif i == 8:
        print(1)
        print(10)
        print(' '.join(['5']*10))
    elif i == 9:
        print(1)
        print(8)
        print(' '.join(['-3']*8))
    elif i == 10:
        print(1)
        print(10)
        print(' '.join(map(str, range(1,11))))
    elif i == 11:
        print(1)
        print(10)
        print(' '.join(map(str, range(10,0,-1))))
    elif i == 12:
        print(1)
        print(5)
        print('2 3 5 7 11')
    elif i == 13:
        print(2)
        print(1)
        print(5)
        print(1)
        print(-5)
    elif i == 14:
        print(1)
        print(3)
        print('0 0 0')
    elif i == 15:
        print(1)
        print(4)
        print('-1 0 1 2')
    elif i == 16:
        print(5)
        print(1)
        print(10)
        print(2)
        print('-5 5')
        print(3)
        print('1 2 3')
        print(4)
        print('4 3 2 1')
        print(5)
        print('0 0 0 0 0')
    elif i == 17:
        print(3)
        print(3)
        print('-2 -1 0')
        print(6)
        print('1000 -1000 500 -500 250 -250')
        print(2)
        print('7 7')
    elif i == 18:
        print(1)
        print(15)
        print('12 -7 3 0 -1 8 6 -4 9 -2 5 -3 11 -6 4')
    elif i == 19:
        print(2)
        print(15)
        print(' '.join(map(str, [-1000,999,-998,997,-996,995,-994,993,-992,991,-990,989,-988,987,-986])))
        print(15)
        print(' '.join(map(str, range(-5,10))))
